summary: Excellent audience tape with crystal‑clear sound that showcases a scorching Scarlet > Fire transition and standout West LA solo.
review: The recording is a very nice AUD capture – clear, well‑balanced and lively, with only a minor drum‑mix quirk on the Fire segment. Musically the show shines: Jerry’s guitar work (especially the West LA solo) is on fire, Brent’s organ/scat moments sparkle, and the Scarlet > Fire jam is repeatedly called one of the greatest moments of the night.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews are overwhelmingly positive (mostly 4‑5 stars), praising both the musical performance and the audio quality; only minor technical complaints appear.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced audience tape with good instrument separation; a single comment notes drum sound on Fire being a bit off, but overall the audio draws the listener in.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note an unusual first‑set bookend but agree the Scarlet > Fire sequence anchors the show and leads to a strong, rock‑filled conclusion.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his guitar tone, soaring solos (West LA) and vocal performance; reviewers say "Jerry was definitely on this evening".
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ solo and scatting are highlighted as “bad ass”; his work in the Scarlet > Fire transition receives special praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drum work gets accolades for bend‑out sounds, a great drum segment, and a unique talking‑drum moment; one reviewer notes the drum mix on Fire was a bit whack.
